Bash(curl -s "https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/data/IMF.STA,CPI,5.0.0/A.ES.PCPIHA_PC_PP_PT?startPeriod=2020&endPeriod=2025" | python -c "
import sys
from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
xml = sys.stdin.read\(\)
root = ET.fromstring\(xml\)
ns = {'ss': 'http://www.sdmx.org/resources/sdmxml/schemas/v2_1/data/structurespecific'}
series = root.findall\('.//Series', ns\)
print\(f'Series encontradas: {len\(series\)}'\)
for s in series[:3]:
    print\('Attrs:', s.attrib\)
    for obs in s.findall\('Obs', ns\):
        print\(' Obs:', obs.attrib\)
")

Bash(curl -s "https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/data/IMF.STA,CPI,5.0.0/ESP.HICP.CP00.YOY_PCH_PA_PT.A?startPeriod=2016&endPeriod=2025" | python -c "
import sys
from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
xml = sys.stdin.read\(\)
root = ET.fromstring\(xml\)
series = [e for e in root.iter\(\) if 'Series' in e.tag]
print\('Series:', len\(series\)\)
for s in series:
    print\('Attrs:', {k.split\('}'\)[-1]: v for k,v in s.attrib.items\(\)}\)
    for obs in list\(s\):
        print\(' ', {k.split\('}'\)[-1]: v for k,v in obs.attrib.items\(\)}\)
")

import yaml

    with open\(f'config/profiles/{profile}/frameworks.yaml'\) as f:

Bash(cd "/c/Users/Maria/Documents/github_clone/macro_cosas" && .venv/Scripts/python -c "
import yaml
for profile in ['world', 'europe']:
    with open\(f'config/profiles/{profile}/frameworks.yaml', encoding='utf-8'\) as f:
        cfg = yaml.safe_load\(f\)
    print\(f'--- {profile} ---'\)
    for k,v in cfg.get\('frameworks',{}\).items\(\):
        label = v.get\('label'\) or v.get\('name'\) or k
        print\(f'  {k}: {label}'\)
")

from api.job_store import create_job, get_job, update_job
from datetime import datetime

jid = create_job\({'profile': 'world', 'geos': ['ES']}\)
print\('created:', jid\)

update_job\(jid, status='running', started_at=datetime.utcnow\(\), output_dir='/tmp/test'\)
job = get_job\(jid\)
print\('status:', job['status']\)
print\('started_at type:', type\(job['started_at']\)\)
print\('output_dir:', job['output_dir']\)
print\('OK'\)
")
